headings.text: Ready? Pick one pledge.Start this week.

paragraphs.text: Six evidence-backed pledges, one global protection target, and a timeline of treaties that prove change is possible.
Each pledge below is calibrated against peer-reviewed mitigation pathways. Effort scores reflect the magnitude of measurable impact when adopted at the household, institutional, or policy scale.
Refuse, refill, repair. A typical household avoids ~38 kg of plastic per year by swapping six common single-use items for durable alternatives.
Petition national delegates to ratify the High Seas Treaty and commit to 30×30. Fully protected zones recover biomass by 4–6× within a decade.
Use MSC, ASC, or Seafood Watch certifications. Avoid bluefin, orange roughy, and trawl-caught shrimp. Sustainable choices cut bycatch impact by 70%.
Calculate your annual footprint and offset via verified blue-carbon projects — mangrove and seagrass restoration sequesters 4× more carbon per hectare than forests.
Coral nurseries can outplant 30,000+ fragments per hectare per year. A monthly donation of $15 supports one square meter of recovering reef.
Contribute via Reef Check, eBird Pelagic, or iNaturalist. Volunteer-collected data underpins 22% of peer-reviewed marine biodiversity papers.
Researchers, educators, and policy advisors — share your context and we'll route you to the right working group within 5 business days.
Where individual choice meets systemic policy. Each lever links to evidence-graded pathways in the Deep Current methods archive.

Protect at least 30% of land and ocean by 2030, with effective management of biodiversity hotspots. Today, only 2.9% of the ocean is fully protected from extractive activity — the rest is paper-protected at best.
At current ratification rates, the High Seas Treaty enters force in late 2025. Coastal states must ratify before COP30 to keep the 30×30 timeline credible.
One pledge per person · one habit per month · compounded across 8 billion neighbors.
An interactive infographic atlas of peer-reviewed ocean health data — for climate researchers, educators, and the curious.

Turn data into tide. Six evidence-backed pledges, one global protection target, and a timeline of treaties that prove change is possible. Source consortium: IUCN UNEP NOAA IPCC AR6 UN Treaty Series Marine Conservation Institute Six Pledges · One Ocean Each pledge below is calibrated against peer-reviewed mitigation pathways. Effort scores reflect the magnitude of measurable impact when adopted at the household, institutional, or policy scale. Lifestyle Reduce single-use plastic Refuse, refill, repair. A typical household avoids ~38 kg of plastic per year by swapping six common single-use items for durable alternatives. Take this pledge Effort · LOW Impact · HIGH Policy Support Marine Protected Areas Petition national delegates to ratify the High Seas Treaty and commit to 30×30. Fully protected zones recover biomass by 4–6× within a decade. Sign petition Effort · MED Impact · CRITICAL MSC Diet Eat sustainable seafood Use MSC, ASC, or Seafood Watch certifications. Avoid bluefin, orange roughy, and trawl-caught shrimp. Sustainable choices cut bycatch impact by 70%. Get the guide Effort · LOW Impact · MED Climate Offset your carbon Calculate your annual footprint and offset via verified blue-carbon projects — mangrove and seagrass restoration sequesters 4× more carbon per hectare than forests. Calculate footprint Effort · LOW Impact · HIGH Donate Fund reef restoration Coral nurseries can outplant 30,000+ fragments per hectare per year. A monthly donation of $15 supports one square meter of recovering reef. Donate monthly Effort · LOW Impact · HIGH Volunteer Join citizen science Contribute via Reef Check, eBird Pelagic, or iNaturalist. Volunteer-collected data underpins 22% of peer-reviewed marine biodiversity papers. Find a project Effort · MED Impact · HIGH 30×30 Global MPA Target 8% PROTECTED TARGET · 30% BY 2030 CURRENT 8.16% GAP 21.84% DEADLINE 2030 Fully protected Pledged · pending 30% goal marker Protection Milestones A half-century of treaties 1972 MARPOL Convention Pollution from ships regulated. 1982 UNCLOS adopted Law of the Sea framework established. 1986 Whaling moratorium IWC bans commercial whaling. 1992 Convention on Biological Diversity 196 parties pledge to conserve biodiversity. 2006 Papahānaumokuākea MPA 1.5M km² Pacific monument declared. 2015 Paris Agreement 1.5°C target — ocean implications binding. 2022 Kunming-Montréal 30×30 Global biodiversity framework adopted. 2023 High Seas Treaty First legal framework for international waters. RATIFICATION · IN PROGRESS Stay In Current Brief our team Researchers, educators, and policy advisors — share your context and we'll route you to the right working group within 5 business days. Full name * Institutional email * Role Message * Markdown supported · 1500 char max Subscribe to the quarterly Deep Current briefing — peer-reviewed updates, no marketing. Send to working group By submitting you accept the data handling policy . We never share contact details. Eight Levers of Change Where individual choice meets systemic policy. Each lever links to evidence-graded pathways in the Deep Current methods archive. −38kg Refuse plastic 4× Blue carbon 30% MPA target 22% Citizen data $15 Reef m²/mo K-12 Curriculum 2024 Vote ocean $ $2T Divestment Pledge Adoption · 2024 Self-reported adoption rate across Deep Current community (n = 42,318) Bars indicate the percentage of surveyed participants who have made each pledge in the last 12 months. All indicators trending positive — colored teal per the editorial style guide. 0% 20 40 60 80 100 Reduce single-use plastic 68% Support MPA legislation 41% Sustainable seafood only 54% Offset annual carbon 29% Reef restoration donation 36% Citizen science contribution 19% Vote for ocean-aligned policy 73% avg 45.7% Survey conducted Mar–Jun 2024 across 64 countries · weighted by region. SRC · DC-COMMUNITY-2024 What "30×30" actually means Protect at least 30% of land and ocean by 2030, with effective management of biodiversity hotspots. Today, only 2.9% of the ocean is fully protected from extractive activity — the rest is paper-protected at best. Window is closing At current ratification rates, the High Seas Treaty enters force in late 2025. Coastal states must ratify before COP30 to keep the 30×30 timeline credible. The data does not need our optimism. It needs our action — measured, repeated, and shared. — Dr. Annaliese Cordero, Editor-in-chief · Deep Current Ready? Pick one pledge. Start this week. One pledge per person · one habit per month · compounded across 8 billion neighbors.
